questions.question: Why is gather-then-write the wrong checkpoint pattern at scale?
options: All state passes through rank 0; write bandwidth is the slowest single link, not the aggregate, so a TB-scale write takes hours
questions.explanation: Sharded write scales with cluster size: 64 ranks writing in parallel finish in 1/64 the time of one rank writing the gathered state.

questions.question: What does the manifest record that the shard files cannot?
options: world_size, schema version, per-shard sha256, and shard ownership; gives the loader a contract to validate before touching state
questions.explanation: Without the manifest the loader has to guess which shard belongs to which rank and trust the file count is right.

questions.question: Why write to <name>.tmp and rename instead of writing directly?
options: POSIX rename within a filesystem is atomic: a crash mid-write leaves the previous file untouched. Direct writes can leave half-finished checkpoints that poison the next resume
questions.explanation: Without atomic write a partial save plus an updated manifest pointing at it corrupts state on the next load.

questions.question: What does sha256 per shard defend against?
options: Partial or corrupted writes; verifying the hash on load catches truncated shards before they touch model state
questions.explanation: Without sha256 verification a truncated shard loads silently and corrupts the optimiser, surfacing later as NaN loss.

questions.question: Why does the load path reject a world_size mismatch loudly?
options: Resuming a 4-rank shard layout on 8 ranks silently misassigns shards; loud rejection forces the operator to use the cross-world-size rebalance path
questions.explanation: The cross-world-size rebalance is a separate operation; silently splicing wrong sized shards corrupts state.

questions.question: Why does production rotate checkpoints (keep last K)?
options: Without rotation the disk fills mid-run and the next checkpoint fails; rotation deletes the oldest before writing the new one, bounding disk usage
questions.explanation: Real runs keep 3-5 recent checkpoints; the oldest is dropped before each new save so the disk budget is fixed.
